key_insight: A detailed finite element study was used to refine the WST altitude structure so it can safely support the mirrors while minimizing weight, deformation, stress, and resonance risk.
engineering_relevance: The work provides design criteria and an optimized structural configuration for a large steel telescope mount, directly informing stiffness, mass, and dynamic performance trade-offs in precision astronomical hardware.
method: Built a detailed FE model of the altitude structure with beams and plates and representative boundary conditions.
Evaluated structural weight, mechanical deformations, stresses, and frequency response for local and global modes.
Used preliminary cross sections and shell thickness results to iteratively optimize the structure.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports iterative FE-based evaluation of structural mass, stress, deformation, and modal response, which was used to derive the final refined design.
watch_item: Because the optimization is based on modeled preliminary geometries and boundary conditions, real-world performance still depends on how well the FE assumptions match operational loads and support conditions.
paper_title: WST, the Wide-field Spectroscopic Telescope: Telescope structure FE analyses
